## [00:00 - 01:06]

This is a very good way for learning that I'll give you to you right now. How does a chef become a great chef? Practice? Yes, by copying the recipes from other chefs. That's what people do. Imitation is the greatest form of flattery. What I did when I was learning to become a great speaker was I purchased about 50 books on speaking to really map it out and understand the business of speaking but then also watched a whole bunch of videos of speakers online and I watch them and I imitate. Because a chef, think about it, a chef borrows a recipe, imitates the recipe and then the first time the chef cooks the meal, the novice chef cooks it and goes, that wasn't too bad, I need to follow the method. I'm going to imitate even closer. Takes another recipe, imitates it even closer and it tastes good. What does that do for the chef? Gives him or her confidence. It's like, oh, I can cook and as they build confidence, what do they do? They add in a little bit of garlic, a little bit of spices and they make it their own. So when you look at me, I'm the combination of about six speakers that I really admire. I watched and I started to see all the foundations. I started to see the vocal archetype range. Started to see the humor. Huh, copy them. And then after that, when I became confident, I made it my own.
